input.prompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a photo viewer page in my .NET MAUI app targeting .NET 10. I want
the image to fill the entire screen edge-to-edge, going behind the status bar
and navigation bar. But my image still has padding around it even though I set
SafeAreaEdges="None" on the ContentPage. What am I missing?

data.content:
`SafeAreaEdges` isn't what controls this in .NET MAUI. You need two things:

**1. Set `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on your layout or view:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False">
    <Grid IgnoreSafeArea="True">
        <Image Source="photo.jpg"
               Aspect="AspectFill" />
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

`IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the `Grid` (or any `Layout`) tells MAUI to extend content into the safe area insets (status bar, navigation bar, notch areas).

**2. On Android, make system bars translucent** (in `MainActivity.cs` or `Platforms/Android`):

```csharp
Window.SetStatusBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);
Window.SetNavigationBarColor(Android.Graphics.Color.Transparent);

// For .NET 10 / Android 15+, edge-to-edge is enforced by default,
// but for older targets:
if (OperatingSystem.IsAndroidVersionAtLeast(30))
{
    Window.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(false);
}
```

**Key points:**
- `IgnoreSafeArea` is the MAUI property that does the work — it's on `Layout`, not `Page`
- On **iOS**, this extends under the status bar and home indicator automatically
- On **Android**, you also need transparent system bars so content shows through
- Hide the nav bar with `NavigationPage.HasNavigationBar="False"` (or Shell equivalent) if you don't want the MAUI navigation bar
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about making content go edge-to-edge in a .NET MAUI app, going behind the status bar and navigation bar. They've tried `SafeAreaEdges="None"` but still have padding.

This is a common .NET MAUI question. Let me think about what they need:

In .NET MAUI, to achieve true edge-to-edge content:

1. `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on the layout/view itself is the key property they're likely missing
2. On Android, you may also need to opt into edge-to-edge mode
3. On iOS, `SafeAreaEdges` on the page controls whether the page respects safe areas, but individual views may still need `IgnoreSafeArea`


4. For iOS specifically, there's also the `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="false"` platform-specific approach
5. On Android 15 and later, edge-to-edge handling has changed, so they may need to account for that depending on their target API level

The most likely culprit is that they haven't set `IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on their root layout or the specific view they want to extend edge-to-edge.
